title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
At the "Happy Coffee House" in London, Polly attempts to persuade a waitress named Fay Spoon to recall any memories regarding Jenny. Fay claims she cannot remember anything more, though she mentions that a polite man recently visited the shop accompanied by a policeman. During their conversation, Poirot observes that Fay believes a man had successfully imitated his voice, though Poirot maintains that every voice and rhythm is unique.

Raphael Buckle, a waiter from the Blaukenshahn Hotel, arrives at the coffee house and explains that Luca Lazzari directed employees to find Poirot at this location. Buckle provides new recollections regarding the night of the crime, specifically a conversation involving Harriet Seppel, Ada Gransbury, and Richard Nigges. Buckle remembers Mrs. Hepburn making eerie laughter and comments about a woman being "old enough to be his mother" and a man no longer trusting her. He also recalls Mr. Nigges disagreeing with the age comment and questioning if a certain man "has a brain."

After Buckle departs, Poirot returns to Blanche Onsworth's apartment while pondering these statements. Upon arrival, Blanche informs him that a ragged man named Samuel Kid is waiting in the sitting room. She notes that the man's face is covered in bruises, which he claimed were shaving cuts. Poirot, remembering meeting Kid previously when he had only a partially shaved beard, rushes to the parlor. He finds that Samuel Kid has shaved his face into a state of disarray to deceive Blanche into letting him in.
